free_text: Yields of products with A=100-117 in the interaction
Of 3- and 28-GeV protons with uranium and lead
The quoted errors were based solely on the
agreement between duplicate experiments. The actual
errors were in many intances larger, primarily because
of uncertainties in the decay schemes and in the
resolution of the photopeaks.
Data from PR,148,1235,1966--tables I and II.
Bib corrections.
Energy, decay data corrections.
Converted to lower case.
OS. Some isomer assignments corrected
in subs.2,3,6,7.
The targets consisted of stacks of aluminum and
uranium foils having a thickness of 0.001 and
0.001 in., respectively.
Comment by compiler: The 4.9 hr activity of In-110
was assigned by authors to In-110m
Isomer assignment for Pd-109 and In-110
corrected in DECAY-DATA and/or ISOMER
